CRITICAL - Element Identification:
- Use the "selector" field to find the target element (contains CSS selector like "#id", ".class", or tag)
- "elementPath" contains the DOM path (e.g., "html > body > div > ...") - use to understand element hierarchy
- "elementHTML" shows the element's HTML structure for verification
- "elementClasses" lists all CSS classes on the element - search for these in stylesheets
- If "elementReactComponent" is provided, the component name (e.g., "Button", "Card") indicates where to look

CRITICAL - Where to Make Changes:
1. First check if the element uses utility classes (Tailwind, Bootstrap): modify the className/class attribute in JSX/TSX
2. For CSS Modules (*.module.css): find the imported module and update the corresponding class
3. For styled-components/emotion: find the styled() or css`` definition in the component file
4. For global CSS/SCSS: locate the stylesheet imported by the component or in global styles
5. For inline styles: update the style prop directly in the JSX/TSX file

CRITICAL - Making the Actual Change:
- "property" is the CSS property name (e.g., "color", "padding", "font-size")
- "oldValue" is the current value - use this to locate the exact line to change
- "newValue" is what it should be changed to
- Preserve CSS units and format (e.g., if oldValue was "16px", keep pixel units unless converting to rem/em)

For CSS Style Changes:
1. Search for the element using "selector" or "elementClasses" to find relevant stylesheets
2. Find the exact CSS rule containing "property: oldValue"
3. Replace "oldValue" with "newValue"
4. If no existing rule exists, add a new declaration to the appropriate CSS file or inline style
5. For Tailwind: convert the CSS property/value to the appropriate utility class (e.g., "padding: 16px" → "p-4")
6. Ensure specificity is maintained - don't accidentally override other rules

You can absolutely do that, and you already have the right assets: `/icons/house.webp`, `/icons/hotel.webp`, `/icons/skyscraper.webp`.

I’m in Ask mode, so I can’t apply edits directly, but this is the exact place to change:

```1514:1530:src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx
function groupTooltipForPlayer(playerId: string, group: ColorGroup): string {
  const tiles = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) => t.type === 'property' && t.group === group);
  const lines: string[] = [];
  const state = store.getState();
  for (const t of tiles) {
    const ps = propsState.byTileId[t.id];
    const owned = ps?.ownerId === playerId;
    if (!owned) continue;
    const imp = ps?.improvements ?? 0;
    const impText = improvementLabel(imp);
    const rent = computeRent(state, t.id, 0);
    const rentText = rent > 0 ? ` — Rent $${rent}` : '';
    lines.push(`${t.name}${imp > 0 ? ` — ${impText}` : ''}${rentText}`);
  }
  if (lines.length === 0) return '';
  return lines.join('\n');
}
```

Use a ReactNode tooltip instead of plain text, like this:

```tsx
function renderImprovementIcons(imp: number): React.ReactNode {
  if (imp <= 0) return null;
  if (imp >= 6) return <img src="/icons/skyscraper.webp" alt="Skyscraper" className="h-4 w-4" />;
  if (imp === 5) return <img src="/icons/hotel.webp" alt="Hotel" className="h-4 w-4" />;
  return (
    <>
      {Array.from({ length: imp }).map((_, i) => (
        <img key={i} src="/icons/house.webp" alt="House" className="h-4 w-4" />
      ))}
    </>
  );
}

function groupTooltipForPlayer(playerId: string, group: ColorGroup): React.ReactNode {
  const tiles = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) => t.type === 'property' && t.group === group);
  const state = store.getState();

  const rows = tiles
    .filter((t) => propsState.byTileId[t.id]?.ownerId === playerId)
    .map((t) => {
      const imp = propsState.byTileId[t.id]?.improvements ?? 0;
      const rent = computeRent(state, t.id, 0);
      return (
        <div key={t.id} className="flex items-center gap-1">
          <span>{t.name}</span>
          {imp > 0 && (
            <>
              <span>-</span>
              <span className="inline-flex items-center gap-0.5">{renderImprovementIcons(imp)}</span>
            </>
          )}
          {rent > 0 && <span>- Rent ${rent}</span>}
        </div>
      );
    });

  return rows.length > 0 ? <div className="space-y-0.5">{rows}</div> : '';
}
```

That gives exactly your format, e.g.:
`[PropertyName] - [houseImg] [houseImg] [houseImg] - Rent [value]`.

If you switch back to Agent mode, I can implement this for you in one pass.

Yep — the exact property-improvements tooltip path is:

1. **Tooltip content is built here** in `PlayConsole`:
```1520:1545:src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx
function groupTooltipForPlayer(playerId: string, group: ColorGroup): React.ReactNode {
  const tiles = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) => t.type === 'property' && t.group === group);
  const rows: React.ReactNode[] = [];
  const state = store.getState();
  for (const t of tiles) {
    const ps = propsState.byTileId[t.id];
    const owned = ps?.ownerId === playerId;
    if (!owned) continue;
    const imp = ps?.improvements ?? 0;
    const rent = computeRent(state, t.id, 0);
    rows.push(
      <div key={t.id} className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1">
        <span className="shrink-0">{t.name}</span>
        {imp > 0 && (
          <>
            <span className="shrink-0">-</span>
            <span className="inline-flex items-center gap-0.5 shrink-0">{renderImprovementIcons(imp)}</span>
          </>
        )}
        {rent > 0 && <span className="shrink-0">- Rent ${rent}</span>}
      </div>,
    );
  }
  if (rows.length === 0) return '';
  return <div className="space-y-0.5">{rows}</div>;
}
```

2. **House/hotel/skyscraper images are chosen here**:
```1508:1518:src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx

}
if (imp === 5) {
  return <img src="/icons/hotel.webp" alt="Hotel" className="h-4 w-4 shrink-0 inline-block align-middle" loading="lazy" decoding="async" />;
}
return Array.from({ length: imp }).map((_, idx) => (
```

---

Short answer: **no core data risk** if you abbreviate only in tooltip display.

Why:
- Game logic keys off tile `id`, `type`, `group`, indexes, and rent selectors — not `name` text.
- In your code, the tooltip is built from `t.name` for display only in `groupTooltipForPlayer` in `src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx`.
- I don’t see property ownership/rent logic doing string comparisons on names.

What can be affected:
- **UI consistency only** (some screens show “Avenue”, tooltip shows “Ave.”).
- **Event/message text** if you abbreviate globally.
- Possible **snapshot tests** (if you have any) that assert exact text.

Best practice:
- Keep canonical names in `BOARD_TILES`.
- Abbreviate at render time (tooltip-only), e.g. a small helper like:
  - `name.replace(/\bAvenue\b/g, 'Ave.')`

So yes — tooltip-only abbreviation is safe from a data integrity standpoint.

Yes — this is a good fit, and it can be done mostly in one place: `src/components/molecules/HudBar.tsx`.

Since you’re in Ask mode, I can’t apply it directly right now, but here’s the clean implementation approach:

## Where to change

- **Main file:** `src/components/molecules/HudBar.tsx`
- **Usage stays same:** `HudBar` is already mounted in `src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx` and already receives all needed values (`busLeft`, `chanceLeft`, `communityLeft`, `housesRemaining`, `hotelsRemaining`, `skyscrapersRemaining`, `depotsLeft`).

## Recommended UI structure

Keep always-visible pills:
- Free Parking
- Bank Owned

Replace individual pills for cards/buildings with 2 collapsible groups:

- **Cards ▾**
  - Bus
  - Chance
  - Community Chest

- **Buildings ▾**
  - House
  - Hotel
  - Skyscraper
  - Depot

## Easiest implementation

Use native `<details>` / `<summary>` inside `HudBar` (no extra dependency), and render each row as icon + label + value.

Example shape:

```tsx
<details className="rounded-lg border border-surface px-2 py-1">
  <summary className="cursor-pointer text-xs font-semibold">Cards</summary>
  <div className="mt-1 space-y-1 text-xs">
    <Row icon="/icons/bus.webp" label="Bus" value={busLeft} />
    <Row icon="/icons/chance.webp" label="Chance" value={chanceLeft} />
    <Row icon="/icons/community.webp" label="Community Chest" value={communityLeft} />
  </div>
</details>
```

and same for `Buildings`.

## Notes

- No data/model changes needed.
- No prop changes needed.
- This is purely `HudBar` presentation refactor.
- If you want chevrons exactly styled, add a tiny `open` state instead of native summary marker (or style the marker off with Tailwind/CSS).

Switch to Agent mode and I’ll implement this directly with your current HUD style.
